channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: 95 victoria road, 13th March
item.description:
Fly tipping 95 victoria road

item.title: Small mattress dumped in courtyard., 13th March
item.description:
We have had some fly tipping, in the Verdant Court courtyard just off Brown Hill Road and Saint Mildred Road. We are requesting respectfully for it to be removed., many thanks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Further Green Road, Catford South, Greater London

item.title: There's a cat litter box and other trash and an old mattress, 13th March
item.description:
Trash and a mattress here behind the pub in a small parking area that, it's keeps flying everywhere because of the wind.
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Hoxton Street, Hackney, London

item.title: Accumulated mixed waste., 13th March
item.description:
Huge amount of accumulated dumped waste and fly tipping amongst planted area near the foot bridge steps. This poses a health hazard.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Oldfield Grove, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter on footpath as a result of people putting out their litter in bags the previous night., 13th March
item.description:
This problem occurs every *single*week and is caused by the same people. It makes a huge mess and remains on the Main Street until others clean it. I’d like the problem addressed at the root cause and stop the people doing it.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lower Road, Lower Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
TV dumped.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Oldfield Grove, Greater London

item.title: This is not a dump, 13th March
item.description:
Can the council check cctv cameras and identify who is dumping stuff in the same location every single day???

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Pownall Road, Hackney, London

item.title: Loose drain cover, 13th March
item.description:
The junction of Wadding st and Content st has a broken drain that keeps banging twice when vehicles go over it, all day and all night, keeping us awake
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Content Street, Walworth, Greater London

item.title: Report of Second Unused Vehicle Parked Adjacent to Previously Reported Vehicle, 13th March
item.description:
I am submitting a separate report regarding a second vehicle parked directly next to the vehicle already reported under registration YP14WVD (the former ambulance). This second vehicle appears to be owned by the same individual and has remained parked in the same location for an extended period without ever being moved. The issues previously raised are therefore compounded by the presence of two unused vehicles occupying street parking. The concerns are as follows: • The vehicle has not moved for a prolonged period and appears to be permanently stored on the public road. • It occupies a residential street parking space despite not being used as an active vehicle. • The long term stationary nature suggests it may effectively be abandoned or being stored on the highway rather than on private property. • The presence of two such vehicles next to each other significantly reduces available parking for residents. To the best of my knowledge, neither vehicle has been moved at any point since being parked there. As noted, a separate report has already been submitted for the vehicle with registration YP14WVD. This report is to highlight the additional vehicle parked immediately beside it, which appears to be under the same ownership and exhibiting the same issues. I would appreciate it if the council could investigate both vehicles in relation to long term street storage or potential abandonment.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Invicta Works, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dangerous pothole, 13th March
item.description:
There's a Pothole/ road damage on the Roundabout at Harrow Manorway / Yarnton Way (heading from yarnton way towards Lidl and thamesmead). There is a very deep, dangerous pothole on the roundabout near the Lakeside Medical Practice , I have struck this twice now while driving towards Lidl Abbeywood. The impact is severe enough to cause a loss of control or significant vehicle damage. This is a high-traffic area near the GP surgery and the station. It needs an emergency inspection and repair before it causes a serious accident or claims are filed against the council.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): A2041, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
